description: Steps extracted from the implementation plan that are needed to build and verify the Express/MongoDB backend service.

steps.title: Create root folder and .gitignore
actions: Create the project root folder.
Create a root .gitignore file containing node_modules/ and .env.

steps.title: Initialize the backend
actions: Create the backend folder.
Initialize npm.
Install runtime dependencies.
Install nodemon as a development dependency.
commands: npm install express mongoose nanoid dotenv cors

steps.title: Create backend environment files
actions: Create backend/.env for local secrets and runtime configuration.
Create backend/.env.example with the same keys for documentation.

steps.title: Add npm scripts
actions: Add start and dev scripts to backend/package.json.

steps.title: Create docker-compose.yml
actions: Create docker-compose.yml at the project root, not inside backend/.
Define a MongoDB 7 service named mongo with port 27017 exposed and a persistent volume.

files.content:
version: "3.8"
services:
  mongo:
    image: mongo:7
    container_name: urlshortener-mongo
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "27017:27017"
    volumes:
      - mongo_data:/data/db

volumes:
  mongo_data:

steps.title: Start MongoDB
actions: Run Docker Compose from the project root.
Verify the urlshortener-mongo container is running.

steps.title: Create backend/models/Url.js
actions: Define a Mongoose Url schema.
Ensure shortCode is unique and indexed for fast lookups.
Use Mongoose timestamps or a manual createdAt default.

steps.title: Create backend/middleware/adminAuth.js
actions: Read the x-admin-key header from each request.
Compare the header value to process.env.ADMIN_API_KEY.
Call next() when the key matches.
Return 401 Unauthorized with a JSON error when the key is missing or invalid.

functions.name: createShortUrl
requirements: Read originalUrl from req.body.
Validate originalUrl with the built-in URL constructor inside try/catch.
Reject invalid URLs with 400 and { error: "Invalid URL" }.
Allow only http:// and https:// protocols.
Generate a short code with nanoid(7).
Check for generated-code collisions in MongoDB and retry until unique.
Save the URL document to MongoDB.
Return 201 with { shortUrl: BASE_URL + "/" + shortCode, shortCode }.

functions.name: createCustomShortUrl
requirements: Read originalUrl and customSlug from req.body.
Validate originalUrl using the same rules as createShortUrl.
Validate customSlug with /^[a-zA-Z0-9-]+$/ and length 3 to 30 characters.
Reject reserved slugs: admin, shorten, api.
Return 409 Conflict if customSlug already exists in MongoDB.
Save with customSlug set to true.
Return 201 with { shortUrl, shortCode: customSlug }.

functions.name: redirectToUrl
requirements: Read code from req.params.
Find the matching document by shortCode.
Return 404 with a simple HTML page saying Short URL not found when missing.
When found, increment clicks by 1 and update lastAccessed to Date.now().
Save the document and redirect with status 302 to originalUrl.

functions.name: getAllUrls
requirements: Query all documents from the Url collection.
Support optional sort query values clicks and createdAt.
Default sorting is createdAt descending.
Return 200 with an array of URL documents.

functions.name: getUrlStats
requirements: Read code from req.params.
Find the document by shortCode.
Return 404 when not found.
Return 200 with originalUrl, shortCode, clicks, lastAccessed, createdAt, and customSlug.

functions.name: deleteUrl
requirements: Read code from req.params.
Find and delete by shortCode.
Return 404 when not found.
Return 200 with { message: "Deleted successfully" }.

functions.name: updateUrl
requirements: Read code from req.params and originalUrl from req.body.
Validate the new originalUrl using the same rules as createShortUrl.
Find by shortCode and update originalUrl.
Return 404 when not found.
Return 200 with the updated document.

functions.name: getGlobalStats
requirements: Calculate totalUrls as the count of all documents.
Calculate totalClicks as the sum of all clicks fields.
Return topUrls as the top 5 documents sorted by clicks descending.
Return recentUrls as the last 5 documents sorted by createdAt descending.
Return 200 with all stats in one JSON object.

steps.title: Create backend/routes/admin.js
actions: Apply adminAuth middleware to all routes in this file using router.use(adminAuth).

steps.title: Create backend/server.js
actions: Load dotenv.
Connect to MongoDB using MONGO_URI.
Log MongoDB connection success or exit the process on connection failure.
Initialize the Express app.
Add express.json() middleware for JSON body parsing.
Add cors() middleware so separate frontend pages can call the API from another origin.
Mount admin routes at / before public routes.
Mount public routes at / after admin routes.
Add a global error handler as the last middleware that logs errors and returns 500 { error: "Internal Server Error" }.
Start listening on process.env.PORT.
importantNotes: Mount the admin router before the public router so /:code does not swallow /admin/* routes.
Do not use express.static; the backend is API-only.
All backend routes return JSON except /:code, which redirects or returns a simple not-found HTML page.

steps.title: Verify backend security requirements
checks: ADMIN_API_KEY is read from backend/.env only and is never hardcoded.
All /admin/* routes are protected by adminAuth middleware.
URL validation rejects non-http/https protocols in createShortUrl and createCustomShortUrl.
Custom slugs are validated against a regex and arbitrary strings are rejected.
Reserved slugs admin, shorten, and api are blocked.
shortCode has unique: true and index: true in the MongoDB model.
nanoid collisions are handled with a retry loop.
The global error handler catches unhandled errors and does not leak stack traces to clients.
cors middleware is enabled in backend/server.js.
